title: papaver stewartianum
intro: Papaver stewartianum, also known as Stewart's poppy, is a perennial plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the western Himalayas. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Papaver stewartianum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is not widely documented and should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
